task_completion.description: Log in to Moodle as the admin user.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moodle dashboard is visible with the admin user profile (e.g., 'Admin User') displayed in the top right corner.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Computer Science Fundamentals' (CS110) course.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The course page is visible, indicated by the header 'Computer Science Fundamentals' or the shortname 'CS110' in the breadcrumbs or title.

task_completion.description: Initiate the creation of a new Choice activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings page titled 'Adding a new Choice' or similar is visible, showing the general configuration form for a Choice activity.

task_completion.description: Enter the correct name and description for the Choice activity.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Choice name' field contains 'Preferred Programming Language' and the 'Description' field contains 'Choose your preferred programming language for the course project. This will help us form balanced project teams.'

task_completion.description: Enter the four specified programming language options.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Options' section of the form, the fields for Option 1, Option 2, Option 3, and Option 4 contain 'Python', 'Java', 'C++', and 'JavaScript' respectively.

task_completion.description: Configure the update and result publishing settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Under the 'Results' section of the form, 'Publish results' is set to 'Always show results to students' and 'Allow choice to be updated' is set to 'Yes'.

task_completion.description: Save the activity and display the final Choice page.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final view of the created Choice activity is shown, displaying the title 'Preferred Programming Language', the description, and radio buttons for the four programming language options.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser GUI to interact with Moodle.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the Moodle interface, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or direct database queries.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard Moodle UI elements (navigation drawer, breadcrumbs, standard theme) are present and functional in the screenshots.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass the UI creation process.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent writing or executing a Python or SQL script to insert the choice activity directly into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must create a Choice activity in the 'Computer Science Fundamentals' (CS110) course. The activity name must be exactly 'Preferred Programming Language'. The description must be 'Choose your preferred programming language for the course project. This will help us form balanced project teams.'. Exactly four options must be provided: 'Python', 'Java', 'C++', and 'JavaScript'. In the activity settings (typically under the 'Results' section), 'Allow choice to be updated' must be set to 'Yes', and 'Publish results' must be set to 'Always show results to students'.
